steps.title: Step 1 — Identify the task
steps.content: Command-of-Evidence question: pick the survey quotation that best supports the students' conclusion that the garden benefits the community by fostering connections between VOLUNTEERS and OTHER residents who weren't volunteering.

steps.title: Step 2 — Match the evidence
steps.content: The correct quote must show volunteers interacting with non-volunteer neighborhood residents. Choice B describes volunteers consulting expert gardeners who live in the neighborhood and notes the experts are eager to help and learn — a direct connection between volunteers and non-volunteer residents.

steps.title: Step 3 — Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'deciding what plants would be most suitable': describes a horticultural/logistical challenge; no interaction with non-volunteer residents. ✗
  • Choice C — 'opportunity to be outside and around nature': personal benefit to the volunteer; no community connection mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the feeling of pride I get when I walk by': individual emotional payoff; doesn't involve other residents. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
